message:
fix(search/query + tests): tantivy writer lock scoping + let-chain cleanup + segment-count doc

tests/search_latency_under_indexing.rs
  The two #[ignore] latency tests opened a TantivyIndex for seeding
  and then tried to open a background IndexWriter further down the
  same function. Tantivy serializes writers via a filesystem lockfile
  — only one IndexWriter may exist per directory at a time — so the
  second open was racing the first writer's Drop and occasionally
  failing with a lock-held error on slower hosts. Wrapping the
  seeding writer in its own scope forces the lock to drop before the
  background indexer starts, which is what the test was always
  implicitly assuming. Applied to both
  search_p95_stays_within_budget_while_indexing_in_background and
  governor_disabled_run_matches_idle_baseline with identical rationale
  comments so the next reader doesn't "simplify" the braces back out.

src/search/query.rs
  Merge an `else if let Some(...) { if let Some(...) { ... } }`
  ladder into an `else if let ... && let ...` chain. Pure clippy /
  style cleanup — no semantic change, just one less level of nesting
  on the federated-readers reload path.

src/indexer/mod.rs
  LexicalRebuildFinalMergeArtifact::segments is only consumed by
  regression tests that prove the final-merge segment frontier is
  not silently remerged; production code paths take only path +
  doc count. #[allow(dead_code)] with a comment so the next cargo
  clippy pass doesn't propose deleting the field (which would then
  force the regression tests to reach into internals the wrong way).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -9650,6 +9650,10 @@ fn publish_staged_lexical_index(staged_index_path: &Path, index_path: &Path) ->
 struct LexicalRebuildFinalMergeArtifact {
     publish_path: PathBuf,
     docs: usize,
+    // Kept for test assertions + future diagnostics; production code only
+    // consumes the path + doc count, but regression tests still read this to
+    // prove the segment frontier is not silently remerged.
+    #[allow(dead_code)]
     segments: usize,
 }
